summary: Implement minimal M4 campaign objectives and planning knowledge model
acceptance_criteria: Authoritative sim-core state represents a minimal CampaignPlan with stable branded IDs, objective target, start window, commander or polity owner, current status, and explicit reason codes.

Campaign objectives are limited to M4 vertical-slice needs: prepare, march, besiege or relieve, withdraw or cancel, and produce a postwar-result candidate; no full diplomacy, treaty, rebellion, or manual battle mode is introduced.
Player and AI actors create, update, and cancel campaign objectives through the same versioned GameCommand paths with explicit serializable payload schemas.
A minimal FactionKnowledge snapshot records known objective, route, supply, and defender estimates with deterministic source/version metadata and does not grant hidden omniscience to UI code.
Planning queries provide forecast/reason fields without mutating WorldState or relying on React, Pixi, Electron, DOM, Node IO, real time, Math.random, or unspecified ordering.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover stable campaign IDs, objective validation, player/AI command parity, deterministic FactionKnowledge ordering, and read-only planning queries.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-CAMPAIGN-OBJECTIVES-001
notes: This task creates only the M4 objective/knowledge substrate; it must not absorb muster, supply, march, siege, UI, or validation work.
Stop and route to systems_architect if objective modeling requires a save-format, protocol-compatibility, or frozen-decision ADR beyond the task scope.

Explicit exclusions for M4 remain: manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product or architecture changes.
Promoted to READY after M4-TASK-GRAPH-001 entered origin/main and was CLOSED.
